message:
feat: Add Q-Healing safety features (corpus, hardware, groggy state)

Three critical safety additions for Q-Healing on consumer Mac M2 hardware:

1. Dream Corpus (The Fuel)
   - Reference corpus mixing (50% corpus + 50% user data)
   - Prevents overfitting during Q-Healing calibration
   - Supports wiki, code, reasoning, and custom corpus sources
   - Storage: ~/.cache/zell/corpus/

2. Hardware Safety (The Brakes)
   - Blocks deep sleep on battery power
   - Blocks on high thermal pressure (serious/critical)
   - Rate limits to 1 deep sleep cycle per 24 hours (SSD protection)
   - Uses macOS pmset and sysctl for monitoring
   - 100ms caching for efficiency

3. Groggy State (Wake-Up Latency)
   - Transitional state: deep_sleep → groggy → awake
   - Prevents frozen UI when interrupting deep sleep
   - Shows "Waking up..." message during layer memory cleanup
   - 10s hard timeout to prevent stuck state

Files:
- src/dream_corpus.zig: Corpus loading, mixing, sampling (~400 lines)
- src/hardware_monitor.zig: Power, thermal, rate limiting (~490 lines)
- src/hippocampus.zig: Groggy state + hardware/corpus integration
- src/dashboard.zig: Groggy display + HARDWARE panel
- src/scheduler.zig: Groggy timeout check in step()
